name: Adam
title: The First Man
summary: The first human being, created by Allah from clay. Allah taught him the names of all things and commanded the angels to prostrate before him. He lived in Paradise with his wife Hawwa (Eve) until they were tempted by Shaytan and sent to earth.

verses.context: Creation and the fall from Paradise
verses.image_prompt: A lush garden paradise with flowing rivers, fruit trees, and golden light filtering through canopy, no people visible, painterly Islamic art style

verses.context: The command to prostrate and Shaytan's refusal
verses.image_prompt: Ethereal beings of light bowing toward the earth in a celestial realm with swirling cosmic clouds, abstract and luminous, no people visible, painterly style
A single tree in a vast garden with one forbidden fruit glowing, a path leading away from the garden toward an earthly landscape below, no people visible, painterly style

name: Nuh
title: Noah — Builder of the Ark
summary: Prophet Nuh called his people to worship Allah alone for 950 years. When they rejected him, Allah commanded him to build an ark. A great flood destroyed the disbelievers while Nuh and the believers were saved on the ark.

verses.image_prompt: Dark storm clouds unleashing torrential rain over rising floodwaters engulfing a village, lightning illuminating a massive wooden ark floating above, no people visible, dramatic painterly style

verses.context: Surah Nuh — his plea to his people
verses.image_prompt: A barren desert village with stone idols standing in a dusty square, overcast sky, signs of neglect and arrogance, no people visible, painterly style
A half-built massive wooden ark on dry land surrounded by timber and tools, clear sky with no water in sight, no people visible, painterly style

name: Ibrahim
title: Abraham — The Friend of Allah
summary: Ibrahim rejected the idol worship of his people and was thrown into a fire which Allah made cool for him. He rebuilt the Ka'bah with his son Ismail and was tested with the command to sacrifice his son, demonstrating ultimate submission to Allah.

verses.image_prompt: The Ka'bah being built from stone blocks in an empty desert valley of Makkah, ancient construction with scattered stones, no people visible, painterly Islamic art style

verses.context: Ibrahim's reasoning about the stars, moon and sun
verses.image_prompt: A night sky transitioning to dawn with brilliant stars fading, a crescent moon setting, and the sun rising over a desert horizon, contemplative and vast, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Destroying the idols and the fire
verses.image_prompt: Shattered stone idols scattered across a temple floor with one large idol still standing, dust and debris, no people visible, dramatic painterly style
A knife laid upon a stone altar on a mountaintop with a ram appearing nearby, dramatic divine light breaking through clouds, no people visible, painterly style

name: Ismail
title: Ishmael — The Patient Son
summary: Son of Ibrahim, left as an infant with his mother Hajar in the barren valley of Makkah. Allah provided the well of Zamzam for them. He helped his father build the Ka'bah and willingly submitted when his father was commanded to sacrifice him.

verses.image_prompt: Ancient stone foundation of the Ka'bah being laid in a barren valley between rocky hills, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: The sacrifice — both submit to Allah
verses.image_prompt: A mountaintop altar with a knife and a ram, golden light from above, peaceful resolution, no people visible, painterly style
A well of clear water (Zamzam) in a desert valley with birds gathering around it, life emerging from barrenness, no people visible, painterly style

name: Yusuf
title: Joseph — The Beautiful Story
summary: Thrown into a well by his jealous brothers, sold into slavery in Egypt, falsely imprisoned, then rose to become a minister of Egypt through his ability to interpret dreams. He forgave his brothers and reunited with his father Yaqub.

verses.context: The entire Surah Yusuf — the most detailed story of any prophet in the Quran
verses.image_prompt: An ancient stone well in a desert landscape with a torn colorful garment draped over its edge, a caravan of camels approaching in the distance, dramatic sky, no people visible, painterly style

name: Musa
title: Moses — The Liberator
summary: The most frequently mentioned prophet in the Quran. Born during Pharaoh's persecution of the Israelites, raised in Pharaoh's palace, called by Allah at the burning bush, confronted Pharaoh with miracles, led the Exodus, received the Torah on Mount Sinai, and guided his people through the wilderness.

verses.context: The burning bush, staff, Pharaoh, and the golden calf
verses.image_prompt: A scene depicting the burning bush, staff, pharaoh, and the golden calf, ancient Middle Eastern landscape, no people visible, painterly Islamic art style

verses.context: Confronting Pharaoh and the parting of the sea
verses.image_prompt: A vast sea parted into two towering walls of water with a dry path between them, dramatic stormy sky above, no people visible, painterly style
A small basket floating gently on the River Nile among reeds, with an Egyptian palace visible in the background, golden morning light, no people visible, painterly style
Dark sky raining hail and fire over an ancient Egyptian landscape with locusts swarming, Nile turned blood-red, no people visible, dramatic painterly style

name: Dawud
title: David — The King-Prophet
summary: Given kingship and prophethood, Allah softened iron for him and taught him to make armor. He was given the Zabur (Psalms). The mountains and birds would glorify Allah with him.

verses.context: Given wisdom and the ability to work iron
verses.image_prompt: A forge with molten iron being shaped into chainmail armor, sparks flying, mountains visible through the workshop opening, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Mountains and birds glorify with Dawud
verses.image_prompt: Mountains at sunrise with flocks of birds in formation, sound waves visually rippling through the air, golden sky, no people visible, painterly style
A throne room with scales of justice, light streaming through high windows, peaceful and contemplative atmosphere, no people visible, painterly style

name: Sulayman
title: Solomon — Lord of the Wind
summary: Son of Dawud, given a kingdom unlike any other. He could command the wind, understood the speech of birds and ants, and had jinn working under his command. He was known for his wisdom and gratitude to Allah.

verses.context: The ant, the hoopoe bird, and the Queen of Sheba
verses.image_prompt: A hoopoe bird carrying a letter in its beak flying over an army of ants on a sunlit path, a distant palace on the horizon, no people visible, painterly style
Massive stone blocks being lifted and placed to build a grand temple, mysterious swirling winds assisting, supernatural construction, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: The wind and the test of the throne
verses.image_prompt: A magnificent ornate throne in a vast hall with wind swirling through open arches, a glass floor reflecting clouds below, no people visible, painterly Islamic art style

name: Yunus
title: Jonah — The One Who Called from Darkness
summary: Left his people in frustration before receiving Allah's permission. Swallowed by a great whale, he called out to Allah from the depths of darkness. Allah forgave him and his people became believers.

verses.context: His prayer from the belly of the whale
verses.image_prompt: The dark interior of a great whale's belly with a faint beam of light filtering through, deep ocean visible outside, ethereal and claustrophobic, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Cast into the sea and swallowed
verses.image_prompt: A great whale surfacing in stormy seas with its mouth open wide, dark churning waters, lightning in the background, no people visible, dramatic painterly style
A barren shoreline where the sea meets land, a single green plant sprouting from the sand providing shade, calm after storm, no people visible, painterly style

name: Isa
title: Jesus — The Miraculous Word
summary: Born miraculously to Maryam (Mary) without a father, spoke from the cradle, healed the blind and lepers, and raised the dead by Allah's permission. He was not crucified but raised up to Allah. He will return before the Day of Judgement.

verses.context: The annunciation and birth of Isa
verses.image_prompt: A single palm tree heavy with ripe dates beside a gentle stream in a peaceful valley, soft divine light, serene atmosphere, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Miracles and the table from heaven
verses.image_prompt: A table descending from the sky laden with food, surrounded by divine light, clouds parting above, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Maryam and the birth — Isa speaks from the cradle
verses.image_prompt: A palm tree with dates falling beside a flowing stream in a remote valley, warm golden light, peaceful and miraculous atmosphere, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: He was not crucified — raised to Allah
verses.image_prompt: A beam of divine light ascending from earth into the heavens through parting clouds, radiant and transcendent, no people visible, painterly style

name: Muhammad
title: The Final Messenger
summary: The seal of the prophets, sent as a mercy to all of mankind. Born in Makkah, received the first revelation in the Cave of Hira, endured persecution, migrated to Madinah, and established the final message of Islam. The Quran was revealed to him over 23 years.

verses.image_prompt: The Cave of Hira on Mount Jabal al-Nour at dawn with golden light streaming through the entrance, rocky mountain landscape of Makkah, no people visible, painterly style
An ornate golden seal pressed into wax on an ancient scroll, surrounded by light, symbolizing finality and completion, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Sent as a mercy to the worlds
verses.image_prompt: A vast landscape showing desert, ocean, mountains, and green fields all under one harmonious sky with soft rain falling, mercy and blessing, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah
verses.image_prompt: The green dome of Madinah at dusk with stars appearing, peaceful cityscape, warm lights, no people visible, painterly style

verses.context: Victory and completion of the message
verses.image_prompt: The Ka'bah surrounded by light with the city of Makkah visible below, triumphant dawn breaking over the horizon, no people visible, painterly style

name: Ayyub
title: Job — The Patient
summary: Tested with the loss of his health, wealth, and family, Ayyub remained patient and devoted to Allah through years of suffering. Allah restored everything to him and more as a reward for his patience.

verses.image_prompt: A barren cracked landscape with a single spring of cool water beginning to flow from a rock, first signs of green growth, no people visible, painterly style
A once-barren landscape now transformed into lush green fields with abundant water, flowers blooming, complete restoration, no people visible, painterly style

name: Hud
title: Sent to the People of 'Ad
summary: Sent to the powerful ancient people of 'Ad who built great monuments and were arrogant in the land. They rejected his message and were destroyed by a furious wind that lasted seven nights and eight days.

verses.image_prompt: Towering ancient stone monuments and grand pillars in a prosperous desert city, impressive but foreboding, no people visible, painterly style
A massive wall of sand and wind approaching an ancient city, buildings beginning to crumble, ominous dark sky, no people visible, dramatic painterly style
Grand palatial ruins with fallen columns and ornate carvings half-buried in sand, remnants of a once-great civilization, no people visible, painterly style
A violent sandstorm engulfing everything in its path, sand dunes being reshaped by furious winds, total devastation, no people visible, painterly style

name: Salih
title: Sent to the People of Thamud
summary: Sent to Thamud, who carved homes from mountains. Allah sent a she-camel as a miraculous sign. Despite warnings, they hamstrung the camel and were destroyed by a thunderous blast.

verses.image_prompt: A lone she-camel drinking from a desert well beside ancient rock-cut dwellings carved into cliff faces, peaceful scene, no people visible, painterly style

A dead she-camel lying beside a dry well in front of crumbling cliff dwellings, desolation setting in, no people visible, painterly style

name: Lut
title: Lot — Righteous Among the Wicked
summary: Nephew of Ibrahim, sent to the people of Sodom who engaged in unprecedented wickedness. Angels came to warn him to leave before the cities were overturned and destroyed by a rain of stones.

verses.context: The sin of his people and their destruction
verses.image_prompt: An ancient city being overturned, buildings flipping upside down, rain of stones falling from a dark sky, total destruction, no people visible, dramatic painterly style

name: Shuayb
title: Sent to the People of Madyan
summary: Called his people to stop cheating in weights and measures and to worship Allah alone. Known for his eloquence. His people rejected him and were destroyed by an earthquake.

verses.image_prompt: An ancient marketplace with overturned scales, scattered goods, and broken measuring weights, signs of dishonesty, no people visible, painterly style
Ground cracking open beneath an ancient trading town, buildings collapsing into fissures, dust clouds rising, no people visible, dramatic painterly style

name: Idris
title: Enoch — Raised to a High Station
summary: Known for his truthfulness and patience. Allah raised him to a high station. He is mentioned briefly in the Quran as a righteous prophet.

verses.context: A man of truth and patience, raised high
verses.image_prompt: A brilliant star ascending through layers of luminous clouds into the heavens, celestial light and cosmic beauty, no people visible, painterly style
A serene mountain peak above the clouds at sunrise, peaceful and elevated, closest to the heavens, no people visible, painterly style

name: Ishaq
title: Isaac — The Promised Son
summary: Son of Ibrahim and Sarah, given as glad tidings when his parents were old. Father of Yaqub. A righteous prophet through whom the line of Israelite prophets continued.

verses.context: The glad tidings of Ishaq's birth

verses.context: Blessed with Ishaq after the sacrifice

name: Yaqub
title: Jacob — The Grieving Father
summary: Son of Ishaq, father of the twelve tribes of Israel including Yusuf. Endured years of grief after losing Yusuf, going blind from weeping, yet never lost faith in Allah's mercy. Reunited with Yusuf in Egypt.

verses.image_prompt: A deep dark well in an empty desert landscape, a torn colorful garment left beside it, tracks leading away, no people visible, painterly style

A colorful shirt being carried by wind across a vast desert toward green fields of Egypt, symbolising reunion and hope, bright light, no people visible, painterly style

name: Harun
title: Aaron — The Helper
summary: Brother of Musa, given prophethood alongside him as his helper and spokesman. He was eloquent in speech. Left in charge of the Israelites while Musa was on Mount Sinai, he tried to prevent them from worshipping the golden calf.

verses.context: Musa asks for Harun as his helper

name: Zakariya
title: Zechariah — The Devoted Elder
summary: A righteous priest who cared for Maryam. In his old age, he prayed for an heir. Allah granted him Yahya despite his advanced age and his wife's barrenness.

verses.context: Caring for Maryam and praying for a son
verses.image_prompt: An ancient prayer niche (mihrab) in a stone temple with warm candlelight and fresh provisions miraculously appearing, peaceful, no people visible, painterly style

A barren tree in a temple courtyard suddenly sprouting green leaves and blossoms, miracle of new life in old age, no people visible, painterly style

name: Yahya
title: John — The First of His Name
summary: Son of Zakariya, given a name no one had borne before. Granted wisdom as a child, he was compassionate, pure, and dutiful to his parents. He confirmed the word of Allah.

verses.image_prompt: A young sapling growing strong beside an ancient river in a wilderness of olive trees, pure morning light, innocence and wisdom, no people visible, painterly style
A scroll of scripture illuminated by divine light in a simple wilderness setting, river nearby, sacred atmosphere, no people visible, painterly style

name: Dhul-Kifl
title: The One of the Portion
summary: Mentioned among the righteous and patient. Scholars differ on his identity, but he is praised in the Quran alongside other prophets for his steadfastness.

name: Ilyas
title: Elijah — Against Baal Worship
summary: Sent to his people who worshipped an idol called Baal. He called them to worship Allah alone. Praised as one of the best of creation.

name: Al-Yasa
title: Elisha — The Chosen
summary: Mentioned among the best of creation alongside other prophets. A righteous prophet who continued the mission after Ilyas.
